title: PCI-DSS 11.3 — Vulnerability programme: overdue scan cadence and unremediated high-severity
exclusions: Does NOT replace the scanner; measures cadence adherence (quarterly for external, internal as required) and remediation SLA for high/critical findings.
evidence: Saved search 'pci_11_3_scan_sla' (daily) emits per scope: scope, last_scan_at, sla_met, open_high_findings, mean_age_days, to compliance_summary.

controlTest.positiveScenario: A scope whose last_scan_at is 100 days ago with 3 open high findings aged 45 days returns sla_met=0.
controlTest.negativeScenario: A scope scanned within cadence with all findings <30 days old is absent.

spl:
index=vm sourcetype IN (tenable:sc:vuln,qualys:host) earliest=-120d
| stats latest(_time) AS last_scan_at count(eval(severity IN ("high","critical") AND state="open")) AS open_high_findings avg(eval(if(state="open" AND severity IN ("high","critical"),now()-first_seen,null))) AS mean_age_s BY scan_scope
| eval mean_age_days=round(mean_age_s/86400,0)
| lookup pci_scan_scope.csv scan_scope OUTPUT cadence_days sla_days
| eval sla_met=if(last_scan_at>=now()-cadence_days*86400 AND mean_age_days<=sla_days,1,0)
| where sla_met=0
| table scan_scope last_scan_at sla_met open_high_findings mean_age_days
description: Produces, per scan scope, the evidence required by PCI-DSS 11.3 — scans are on cadence and high/critical findings are remediated within SLA.
value: Closes the #1 open-finding reason at QSAs — 'the scan history was incomplete'. Continuous measurement is the intended replacement.
implementation: (1) Normalise scanner feeds; (2) maintain pci_scan_scope.csv (quarterly external, per-change internal); (3) schedule UC daily; (4) sla_met=0 opens a P2; (5) quarterly QSA report. Indexes required: `index=vm`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=qualys:host`, `sourcetype=tenable:sc:vuln`.
visualization: Bullet chart of SLA adherence by scope, table of overdue scopes, single value 'scopes overdue'. Key fields: `last_scan_at`, `mean_age_days`, `mean_age_s`, `open_high_findings`, `scan_scope`, `sla_met`.
knownFalsePositives: Immaterial findings whose exception was approved in the risk register show as 'open' — tag state='accepted_risk' and exclude.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=vm` sourcetype=tenable:sc:vuln, qualys:host; scan-scope lookup `pci_scan_scope.csv`..
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Normalise scanner feeds; (2) maintain pci_scan_scope.csv (quarterly external, per-change internal); (3) schedule UC daily; (4) sla_met=0 opens a P2; (5) quarterly QSA report.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=vm sourcetype IN (tenable:sc:vuln,qualys:host) earliest=-120d
| stats latest(_time) AS last_scan_at count(eval(severity IN ("high","critical") AND state="open")) AS open_high_findings avg(eval(if(state="open" AND severity IN ("high","critical"),now()-first_seen,null))) AS mean_age_s BY scan_scope
| eval mean_age_days=round(mean_age_s/86400,0)
| lookup pci_scan_scope.csv scan_scope OUTPUT cadence_days sla_days
| eval sla_met=if(last_scan_at>=now()-cadence_days*86400 AND mean_age_days<=sla_days,1,0)
| where sla_met=0
| table scan_scope last_scan_at sla_met open_high_findings mean_age_days
```

Understanding this SPL

**PCI-DSS 11.3 — Vulnerability programme: overdue scan cadence and unremediated high-severity** — Closes the #1 open-finding reason at QSAs — 'the scan history was incomplete'. Continuous measurement is the intended replacement.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=vm` sourcetype=tenable:sc:vuln, qualys:host; scan-scope lookup `pci_scan_scope.csv`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Add-on for Tenable (4060). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: vm.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=vm, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `stats` aggregates events by **scan_scope**, producing one row per unique combination for trend analysis and threshold evaluation.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **mean_age_days** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• `eval` defines or adjusts **sla_met** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where sla_met=0` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: last_scan_at, mean_age_days, mean_age_s, open_high_findings, scan_scope, sla_met.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bullet chart of SLA adherence by scope, table of overdue scopes, single value 'scopes overdue'.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for produces, per scan scope, the evidence required by pci-dss 11.3 — scans are on cadence and high/critical findings are remediated within sla. This matters because it catches problems that could lead to audit findings or compliance gaps.
